summary: Half + Full Pallet pricing E2E test. 30/30 unit tests pass (test_pallet_pricing.py + test_pallet_tile_serializer.py). 5/6 live API tests pass. Storefront PDP at /shop/collection/TestSeries 60x60cm works: 3 chips render with correct £/m² + min m², Full Pallet click snaps qty=32 + £26.09/m² + min-notice, m²-minus respects pallet floor, m² chip preserves higher qty, Add-to-Cart writes pallet_tier='full_pallet' to localStorage. CRITICAL: PUT /api/products/{id} does NOT persist m2_per_pallet, m2_per_half_pallet, half_pallet_price — wrong handler is being hit.

critical.issue: New pallet fields (m2_per_pallet, m2_per_half_pallet, half_pallet_price) are silently dropped. Route resolution hits /app/backend/server.py:3818 (registered first via api_router) which uses a LOCAL ProductUpdate model defined in /app/backend/server.py:483 — that model does NOT include the new pallet fields. The intended modular route at /app/backend/routes/products.py:377 (which DOES include the fields in ProductUpdate + sync_fields list) is shadowed and never executes. Verified by direct curl: payload {m2_per_pallet:40, m2_per_half_pallet:20, half_pallet_price:25.99} → GET back shows all three as null. Only legacy pallet_enabled and pallet_price persist (because they exist in server.py's local model).

integration_issues.flow: Storefront PDP: Half Pallet chip click
integration_issues.issue: Spec says 'clicking pallet-tier-half sets qty to 16 m², switches price to £27.50/m²'. Observed: when current qty=32 (after a Full Pallet click), clicking Half Pallet leaves qty=32 unchanged instead of snapping to 16. Price/min-notice updates correctly. May be intentional (don't shrink) — please confirm desired behaviour with PM. Full→m² and starting Half-from-low both behave correctly.

action_items: FIX (CRITICAL): The new pallet fields aren't actually persisted via PUT /api/products/{id}. Two fixes possible: (a) add `m2_per_pallet`, `m2_per_half_pallet`, `half_pallet_price`, `half_pallet_price_per_m2`, `pallet_price_per_m2` to the ProductUpdate Pydantic model at /app/backend/server.py:483 AND extend the sync_fields list in /app/backend/server.py:3912-3931 to include those keys; OR (b) remove the duplicate PUT /products/{product_id} route from server.py:3818 so the modular routes/products.py:377 handler (which already handles the new fields) takes over.
Confirm intended behaviour for Half-Pallet chip click when current qty > 16: should it snap DOWN to 16 (per spec wording) or preserve the larger qty (current implementation)?
critical_code_review_comments: Duplicate PUT /products/{product_id} routes in server.py and routes/products.py — stale legacy handler in server.py is winning; routes/products.py code is dead.
server.py:3854 has dead-code condition: `if v is not None or (k in [...] and v is not None)` — second clause can never add anything the first doesn't already.
models/product.py defines new pallet fields, but server.py imports its own ProductUpdate and never picks up the model file. Consider consolidating to a single source of truth.
server.py is 9949 lines — well over the 700-line guideline. Hard to spot route shadowing. Strongly recommend continuing migration to modular routers/.

success_rate.frontend: 92% (storefront PDP working; one spec deviation on Half→Half snap-down)

seed_data_creation: None. Used pre-seeded Alabaster Polished 60x60cm tile (m2_per_pallet=32, m2_per_half_pallet=16, half_pallet_price=27.5, pallet_price=26.09, room_lot_price=28.99). Test target Adhesive 20kg (sku=ADH-001, id=3ab24973-a23e-4805-8164-9186e5888146) was used for PUT verification — has leftover pallet_price=24.99 from failed update; restore-on-success path didn't run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes the route shadowing bug, re-run /app/backend/tests/test_pallet_pricing_live.py (test_update_product_persists_pallet_fields). Also revisit /app/backend/tests/test_pallet_tile_serializer.py::test_admin_sync_fields_list_includes_pallet_fields — that test only checks the modular routes/products.py list and didn't catch the production route shadowing it. Consider adding a route-uniqueness assertion. Storefront PDP and cart context look solid; toast + localStorage write confirmed. /api/admin/pallet-settings GET works and is properly auth-gated. Half-pallet snap-down behaviour is a UX call.
verified_endpoints: GET /api/tiles/products → 200, returns m2_per_pallet, m2_per_half_pallet, half_pallet_price, pallet_price keys on every product
GET /api/admin/pallet-settings → 200 with admin token, returns {pallet_pricing_mode, pallet_trade_extra_discount_pct, allowed_modes}
PUT /api/products/{id} with pallet payload → 200 BUT new pallet fields NOT persisted (CRITICAL BUG)
verified_ui: Chip text: 'Per m² £28.99/m²' / 'Half Pallet £27.50/m² · min 16 m²' / 'Full Pallet £26.09/m² · min 32 m²'
Full Pallet click → qty=32, price=£26.09/m², [data-testid=pallet-tier-min-notice] = 'Minimum order: 32 m². You can add more, but not less.'
Minus button while Full selected → qty held at 32 (floor enforced)
Per m² click after Full → qty preserved at 32 (no down-reset, per spec)
Add to Cart → localStorage tilestation_cart contains pallet_tier:'full_pallet', priceType:'full_pallet', quantity:32, price:26.09; toast 'Added 32m² (Full Pallet) to cart'
rca_of_the_issue: BACKEND PERSISTENCE BUG — Route shadowing. /app/backend/server.py registers PUT /api/products/{product_id} at line 3818 directly on api_router BEFORE the modular_router (which contains the new routes/products.py:377 handler) is included. FastAPI uses first-match routing, so the legacy server.py handler always wins. That handler validates input via the LOCAL `ProductUpdate` defined at server.py:483, which lists `pallet_enabled, pallet_quantity, pallet_price` but is missing `m2_per_pallet`, `m2_per_half_pallet`, `half_pallet_price`, `pallet_price_per_m2`, `half_pallet_price_per_m2`. Pydantic silently drops unknown fields → update_data has no new-pallet keys → $set never writes them → admin form save appears to succeed but new fields are lost. Reproduction: `curl -X PUT -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -d '{"m2_per_pallet":40, "m2_per_half_pallet":20, "half_pallet_price":25.99}' /api/products/<id>` → response & subsequent GET both omit those keys (or return null).
